narrative:
The best way I can describe today's weather is "quiet". Temperatures are slightly below average, winds are light and there is plenty of sunshine this afternoon. Temperatures jump up tomorrow and take another step up Thursday. We remain dry until Friday afternoon when a cold front brings scattered showers and thundershowers, gusty winds and much cooler temperatures Friday night.

Is winter over? This week's Monday Message tackles this question.

Quiet

Last Updated Tue March 24 at 10:28AM by Wayne Presnell

Forecast Discussion

High pressure centered over the Mid-Atlantic provides a cool easterly flow today keeping temperatures slightly below average. The high shifts east Wednesday and allows temperatures to warm a little. Temperatures warm even more Thursday as winds turn to the southwest ahead of a cold front moving in from the northwest.

The cold front passes through Friday. Ahead of the front, temperatures will be well above average with gusty westerly winds. Scattered showers and thundershowers develop Friday afternoon along the front. Temperatures are much cooler behind the front and we'll notice that Friday night. The higher peaks could see some snow before the precipitation ends Friday night.

High pressure settles in Saturday with clearing skies but temperatures about 20 degrees cooler than Friday.

We begin Sunday with freezing temperatures and frost in many areas but the afternoon is a little milder than Saturday. A noticeable jump in temperatures occurs Monday as high pressure shifts east.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
